title: CACOON - 211info
text: BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs. Home visitors help families coordinate care for their children, connect with healthcare, community support, and make sure their child’s health team works together. Helps prepare older youth with special health needs for the transition to adulthood. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Families with children and youth ages 0-21 with chronic health or developmental conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 760-1834 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 766-6835 Main phone Main phone (541) 760-1834 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Benton County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at the Benton County Sunset Building Services: Case/Care Management * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home Based Parenting Education * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Specialized Information and Referral * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY CONNECTS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - HIV CASE MANAGEMENT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM PUBLIC HEALTH - COMMUNICABLE DISEASE PROGRAM SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - HARM REDUCTION PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
